title: Yuki's First Day Friends
synopsis: Yuki feels scared on her first day at a new preschool, but when she discovers the class pet rabbit needs help, she finds the courage to make new friends and feel happy at her new school.

pages.text: Yuki held Mama's hand tight. The new school looked so big and scary. Her tummy felt funny, like butterflies were dancing inside.

illustration.description: A small Japanese girl with short black hair in colorful clips stands nervously outside a bright yellow preschool building. She's wearing a purple backpack and holding her mother's hand tightly. Her expression shows worry and uncertainty. The school has cheerful windows with rainbow decorations and a welcoming entrance with flowers.
illustration.alt: Yuki looking nervous outside her new preschool

pages.text: "I don't want to go in," Yuki whispered. "What if nobody likes me?" Mama gave her a warm hug. "You are brave and kind," Mama said. "The other children will love you."

illustration.description: Yuki's mother, a warm woman with gentle eyes, crouches down to hug Yuki at eye level. Yuki looks sad with tears in her eyes. They're standing in a hallway with colorful artwork on the walls and cubbies with children's belongings. The lighting is soft and comforting with warm yellow tones.
illustration.alt: Mama comforting worried Yuki with a hug

pages.text: In the classroom, Yuki saw children playing everywhere. A boy with curly hair waved at her. A girl with braids smiled big. But Yuki still felt scared and hid behind the teacher.

illustration.description: A bright, colorful classroom filled with diverse children playing. A Black boy with curly hair waves cheerfully, and a Latina girl with long braids smiles warmly. Yuki peeks shyly from behind Ms. Chen, a kind Asian teacher. The room has reading corners, toy bins, and colorful alphabet posters on the walls.
illustration.alt: Yuki hiding behind teacher while other children play

pages.text: "Look!" said the teacher, Ms. Chen. "Our class rabbit, Cocoa, looks sad today. She's hiding in her house and won't come out. Maybe she feels scared too, just like you."

illustration.description: A brown and white rabbit sits curled up in the corner of a large, clear habitat with wooden hiding houses and fresh vegetables. The rabbit's ears are down and she looks timid. Yuki and Ms. Chen look at the habitat with concerned expressions. The classroom setting shows educational posters and children's artwork in the background.
illustration.alt: Cocoa the rabbit hiding sadly in her habitat

pages.text: Yuki felt sorry for Cocoa. She knew how it felt to be scared. "Can I help her feel better?" Yuki asked. She sat by the cage and spoke softly. "It's okay, Cocoa. I'm scared too."

illustration.description: Yuki sits cross-legged on a colorful rug next to Cocoa's habitat, speaking gently with her hand pressed against the glass. Her expression shows empathy and kindness. Cocoa's ears perk up slightly, showing interest. Other children begin to gather around curiously in the background, creating a warm, inclusive scene with soft lighting.
illustration.alt: Yuki talking gently to Cocoa the rabbit

pages.text: Soon, other children came to help. They all talked softly to Cocoa. The brave little rabbit came out! She sniffed their fingers and made them giggle. Yuki felt happy and brave too. She had made new friends helping Cocoa!

illustration.description: A joyful scene with Yuki, the curly-haired boy, the girl with braids, and two other diverse children sitting in a circle around Cocoa's habitat. Cocoa is now out of her hiding spot, standing on her hind legs with her nose pressed to the glass. All the children are smiling and laughing, with bright, happy expressions. The scene is filled with warm, golden light and rainbow colors.
illustration.alt: Yuki and her new friends happily watching Cocoa come out to play

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Yuki feel about going to her new school?
questions.explanation: Yuki felt scared about starting at her new school because everything was new and big.

questions.question: What was wrong with Cocoa the rabbit?
choices: She was hiding and looked sad
questions.explanation: Cocoa was hiding in her house and looked sad, just like how Yuki felt scared.

questions.question: How did Yuki help Cocoa feel better?
questions.explanation: Yuki sat by Cocoa's cage and spoke softly to her, which helped the rabbit feel better.

questions.question: How did Yuki feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: By helping Cocoa and making new friends, Yuki felt happy and brave at her new school.

pages.text: Yuki felt _____ on her first day at the new school.
Cocoa the _____ was hiding in her house.
Yuki spoke _____ to help Cocoa feel better.
